critical.mandate: Read the latest gap analysis report, triage gaps by severity and story, and produce a remediation action table — ADR-039 §10.22.8
Action proposal rules MUST be applied from scripts/lib/gap-triage-rules.js — the single source of truth for the §10.22.8.2 rule table
Sub-workflow invocation and remediation-report emission are OUT OF SCOPE (E19-S27) — this workflow stops at the triage template-output

step.title: Load Gap Report
step.action: Glob {test_artifacts}/test-gap-analysis-*.md to find all gap analysis reports
Sort glob results deterministically (lexicographic on filename) before processing
For each matched file, parse the YAML frontmatter and extract the `date` field
If a file has missing or malformed frontmatter, HALT with error: "Parse error in {filepath} — malformed or missing frontmatter. Fix the file or regenerate with /gaia-test-gap-analysis" (EC3)
Select the file with the most recent `date` value (ISO date string comparison)

If multiple files share the same most-recent date, tie-break on filename (lexicographic ascending — pick the last), and log a warning: "Multiple gap reports share date {date} — using {filename}" (EC2)
If zero files match the glob, HALT with message: "No gap analysis report found — run `/gaia-test-gap-analysis` first" (EC1)
Parse the selected report's markdown body to extract the gap table rows. Each row has at minimum: story_key, gap_type, severity, and description
Store the loaded gaps list and report metadata (filepath, date, total gap count) in workflow context

step.title: Severity Filter
step.action: Default severity filter: critical + high
In YOLO mode: apply the default filter without prompting

In normal mode: present the default filter and prompt the user to adjust (options: critical-only, critical+high, critical+high+medium, all). If the user selects "none", ABORT with message: "No severities selected — nothing to triage" (EC6)
Apply the selected filter to the gap list
Record `filtered_out_count` = (total gaps) - (filtered gaps) in the triage header context

If 0 gaps remain after filtering (EC7): continue to Step 3 with an empty list — the triage table will render with a header note "No gaps match the selected severity filter" and still emit the template-output
If filtered gap count exceeds 50 (EC8): record an INFO note in the triage header: "Report size {N} exceeds 50-gap perf budget — expect >30s runtime"

step.title: Group by story_key
step.action: Build a triage map from the filtered gap list, keyed by story_key
Each triage row contains: story_key, gap_count, gap_types (deduplicated array), proposed_action (empty — filled in Step 4), status (pending)
Stories with multiple gap types collapse into a single triage row with all gap types aggregated
Sort the triage map deterministically by story_key (lexicographic ascending)

step.title: Action Proposal
step.action: For each triage row, resolve the story's current status: 1. First, try to read the story file at {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}-*.md and parse its frontmatter `status` field (source of truth per CLAUDE.md Sprint-Status Write Safety) 2. If the story file does not exist, fall back to sprint-status.yaml and log a warning: "Story file not found for {story_key} — falling back to sprint-status.yaml" 3. If neither source has the story, mark the row as `skip` with reason `story_not_found` (EC4) and continue to next row
Import and invoke the `proposeAction` function from {project-path}/scripts/lib/gap-triage-rules.js for each gap in the row

The rule table (§10.22.8.2): - uncovered-ac + backlog/ready-for-dev → action: append_ac, sub_workflow: /gaia-add-stories - missing-test + done → action: new_story, sub_workflow: /gaia-triage-findings - missing-edge-case + backlog/ready-for-dev → action: append_edge_case, sub_workflow: /gaia-add-stories - unexecuted + any status → action: expand_automation, sub_workflow: /gaia-test-automate - any gap + in-progress/review/blocked → action: skip, skip_reason: "story is {status} — defer remediation" - unknown gap_type → action: skip, skip_reason: "unknown_gap_type" (EC5)

If a row has multiple gap types, apply the rules to each gap individually and collect all proposed actions for the row. The row's proposed_action becomes the highest-priority non-skip action (or skip if all gaps resolve to skip)
Mark each row's status as `proposed` (or `skip` if all actions are skip)

step.title: Triage Table Output
step.action: Render the triage map as a markdown table with columns: story_key | gap_count | gap_types | proposed_action | sub_workflow | status | skip_reason
Add a triage header containing: report source file, report date, severity filter applied, total gaps, filtered gaps, filtered_out_count, and any INFO notes (perf budget, empty filter, etc.)
Save the rendered triage table to {test_artifacts}/fill-test-gaps-triage-{date}.md
In normal mode: present the triage table for user review — the user can approve or modify per-row actions before the file is finalized
In YOLO mode: auto-accept the default proposals and save immediately
